title: Object creation followed by property assignments should use an object initializer
messageFormat: Use an object initializer instead of assigning properties on separate statements.
summary: Creating an object and then setting its members one statement at a time scatters a single logical construction across several lines and leaves the instance in a half-built state in between. An object initializer keeps the whole shape of the new value in one expression.
This rule flags a local that is assigned a freshly created object and immediately followed by member assignments on that same local.
